transcript: aquarium, a lamp for heat, some ice cubes, and a smoke source. Heat up one end of the aquarium with the light source and cool the other end with the ice cubes. This creates areas of high and low pressure. Place something on the aquarium that shields it from outside air. Then introduce some smoke so we can see the air moving. Kids, don't do this without parental permission and assistance. I've seen it done with a burning paper straw pinched at the end opposite the flame. Plastic straws don't work, kids! With the smoke in the aquarium, you can see the air moving.
